title: clarisia ilicifolia var. micranthera
intro: Clarisia ilicifolia var. micranthera, also known as the "clarisia tree," is a plant species within the family Clusiaceae, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic effects. The health benefits of Clarisia ilicifolia var. micranthera are attributed to its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antiparasitic agent. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve the leaves and bark, which can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, offering a natural remedy for various health conditions.
